opp.name: Pupuk_Indo_CLM
opp.next_step: 10% confidence: May 13- no traction with account- BD to KIV Apr 27 meeting with HoP last week. Project still ongoing but no clear compelling event. Still to clarify DC location requirements + local presence Mar 13 KIV with BD. Meet again on RT by April 16 13rd Oct: BD chasing prospect to qualify on next steps. 08th Sep: NS: Request for eco estimation, internal budget approval, tender to be launched by Mar26. Final decision expected by Sep26 18th Aug: Meeting in PSS event by Aug 28. Demo rescheduled to Sep03 8 Aug: Demo scheduled for 18th Aug 2025 31st Jul: CLM initiative still under discovery. To schedule demo in the 2nd week of August.
opp.ais_why:
• Across 600 chars of NextStep covering six dated entries from Jul 2025 to Apr 2026 ‚Äî including HoP meeting, eco-estimation request, internal budget approval cycle ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic or GenAI. Product scope is iContract only (no AI SKUs). Per the 27 Apr 2026 entry: 'meeting with HoP last week. Project still ongoing but no clear compelling event. Still to clarify DC location requirements + local presence.'
• On 8 Sep 2025: 'NS: Request for eco estimation, internal budget approval, tender to be launched by Mar26. Final decision expected by Sep26.' On 31 Jul 2025: 'CLM initiative still under discovery. To schedule demo in the 2nd week of August.' Pupuk Indonesia is a focused CLM-only evaluation with no compelling event, DC-location gating (Indonesia data residency), and a slow internal-budget approval process.
• Confidence is Medium. With iContract as the only product in scope (no AI SKUs) and the entire 600 chars of dialogue being CLM-discovery and DC-location procedural, score 2 (AI Resistant) reflects the focused single-module CLM evaluation with zero AI dimension.

contacts.name: M. Iqbal A.R.
contacts.title: Vice President of Strategic & Evaluation Procurement

items.quote: CFO Indarto Pamungkas and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto are Contact Roles but have never appeared on a call or activity; budget approval is the stated gate
Apr 2026 Next Step: project still ongoing but no clear compelling event
80%/90% figures are Zycus-presented; no buyer baseline on cycle time, manual cost, or penalty exposure captured
Mar 2026 tender target missed; buyer now frames it as next-year second-semester with no firm date

metrics.narrative: The business problem was articulated clearly on the 3 Sep 2025 demo: manual contract drafting in Microsoft Word, multiple manual approval cycles, no standardized clause library, and fragmented collaboration across tools causing delays and inconsistent clause usage. Zycus presented value targets of 80% reduction in contract cycle time, over 90% metadata-extraction accuracy, reduced off-contract expenditure, and fewer penalty/late-payment fees, and the buyer confirmed the solution fulfils their requirements. However, these figures are Zycus-presented benchmarks, not the buyer's own baseline: no current cycle time, manual-hours cost, off-contract leakage, or penalty exposure has been quantified by the relevant stakeholder current-state cost in the buyer's own numbers is the move that would turn a tolerable pain into a budget-justifying business case.

champion.narrative: There is no functioning champion. M. Iqbal A.R. (VP Strategic & Evaluation Procurement, Contact Role: Decision Maker) is a cooperative evaluator: he led the 3 Sep 2025 demo, asked detailed questions, confirmed fit, and committed to send user counts and sample contracts, and he still responds to relationship touches (registered for the Apr 2026 Jakarta roundtable, politely declined Horizon 2026 on 12 Jun 2026). But he has delivered neither committed artifact in 10 months, has not re-engaged on the CLM initiative, has shown no access to the CFO or Deputy CEO, and has not driven the tender or budget internally. That is transactional cooperation, not advocacy. The other Contact Roles (Muhamad Iqbal Syaeful Hakim, Untung Suprayitno, Nugroho Christijanto, Indarto Pamungkas) have not appeared on any call, and partner WGS (Pingadi) has gone quiet since the demo. Building Iqbal into a real champion with economic-buyer access, or finding one, is the prerequisite to any progress.

sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R. on the tender being multi-vendor)
competition.narrative: There is no active head-to-head bake-off today, but the buyer confirmed on 3 Sep 2025 that competition is coming: the award will run through a public tender open to 'other competitor also,' evaluated on technical fit and price. Coupa and GEP Worldwide are named in Salesforce Competitors__c and are the likely tender entrants, though no buyer preference or evaluation of either has been stated. The real current competitor is do-nothing / status quo: the manual Word process is painful but tolerable, and 10 months of deferral show the buyer feels no urgency to launch the tender at all. Zycus's wedge is early-mover advantage: it is sole-sourced into discovery and a validated demo, with Merlin clause-library, bilingual support, SAP integration via iSaaS, and Certinal signing already positioned against the buyer's stated criteria. The risk is that a delayed tender lets Coupa and GEP enter on equal footing.

sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R. confirming Zycus's situation-impact read)
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is a manual, Word-based contract lifecycle: the legal team drafts from scratch, standards vary by commodity, approvals cycle manually, and collaboration is fragmented, producing delays in supplier onboarding and project kickoff, heavy legal workload, and higher risk exposure. The pain owners are the procurement and legal functions represented by the relevant stakeholder. (VP Strategic & Evaluation Procurement). The pain is confirmed and the buyer agreed with Zycus's read of it, but it is clearly tolerable: 10 months after a validating demo there is still no compelling event and no urgency, which is why the initiative keeps slipping. The unresolved risk is inferred cost of inaction, which has never been quantified.

sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (partner Pingadi on PSRE/BSRE; sales director on pricing model)
SF Next_Step__c 27 Apr 2026 (DC location / local presence)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting will run through a formal public tender governed by Indonesian state-owned-enterprise procurement rules, not a direct commercial close. Two compliance mechanics are already flagged: e-signatures must be PSRE/BSRE-certified per the Indonesian Ministry of IT, and DC-location / data-residency requirements plus local-presence questions are unresolved (Next Step, Apr 2026). Pricing is subscription-based per power user (business users and vendors are effectively free), confirmed by the Zycus sales director on 3 Sep 2025. MSA/SOW redlining, InfoSec, and legal channels have not yet been discussed because the deal has not reached commercials. Clearing the e-signature and data-residency questions before the RFP is what creates paper-process visibility.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Indarto Pamungkas, CFO; Nugroho Christijanto, Deputy CEO)
Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R. on budget-then-tender sequence)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped by title but not engaged. CFO Indarto Pamungkas is an OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator) and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto is another Contact Role, so budget authority is named, but neither has appeared on a call or any Salesforce activity. The buyer explicitly made budget the gate on 3 Sep 2025: 'we are preparing for the budget first. After the budget, we are going to have a tender.' As a state-owned enterprise the commercial decision runs through a multi-layer internal budget-approval and public-tender process rather than a single sign-off. The move is to open a thread to the Deputy CEO to sponsor the internal budget case, since the mid-level evaluator has not driven it in 10 months.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer laid out the full SOE decision process on 3 Sep 2025: Pupuk provides power-user estimations, Zycus returns a pricing proposal, Pupuk prepares the internal budget, then a public tender is launched to multiple vendors ('not only yours, Zycus, but other competitor also'), and the vendor is chosen on technical fit plus price. As a state-owned enterprise there are regulations governing service-procurement decisions. Timeline expectations shifted right: originally a Mar 2026 tender with a Sep 2026 decision, now described as a next-year second-semester initiative. The process is well understood; the risk is that Pupuk has not moved past the very first step (returning user counts) in 10 months, so the whole chain is stalled at the front.

sources: Avoma demo 3 Sep 2025 (M. Iqbal A.R.; IT stakeholder; partner Pingadi/WGS)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated the award criteria directly on 3 Sep 2025: the tender will compare vendors on 'technical specifications, scope of work, and also competing in pricing,' and 'the best provider that could give us services and also the pricing will be the winners.' Specific technical criteria surfaced on the same call: bilingual Indonesian-and-English contract handling (single contracts in two languages for international vendors), SAP integration for a company standardized on SAP, PSRE/BSRE-certified e-signature for Indonesian regulatory compliance, support for a decentralized subsidiary structure, and local Indonesian reference customers. These are confirmed buyer-stated criteria, not inferred, and they define what a winning tender response must cover.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 2.0 / Negative is corroborated by the calls: iContract is the only product in scope, no AI SKUs, the buyer wants a single CLM module, and IT questioned how much training Merlin needs. Merlin is embedded in the CLM value story but is not itself a purchase driver here.

Pushing a broader agentic narrative would misread the buyer; the win is CLM depth plus Merlin clause intelligence within iContract.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 16 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15); momentum 52 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 5 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 2 is anchored on the stage win (16), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 5 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling. The CLM evaluation has had no real movement since the 3 Sep 2025 demo; recent buyer touches are event-invitation replies, and the rep records 10% confidence with no traction.

north_star_verdict.math: Re-qualified 24 Apr 2026, so ~70 days in the current Qualified stage; close date 16 Jul 2027 is 378 days out. Last real deal-evaluation touch was the demo 3 Sep 2025 (10 months ago); a positive CLM update call happened 1 Apr 2026 and the buyer replied to event invitations through 12 Jun 2026, but the initiative itself has no traction ('10% confidence' per Next Step 13 May 2026). The Mar 2026 tender target passed with no launch; buyer now frames it as a next-year second-semester initiative. Long runway to close, but momentum is dead and no forcing function exists.
evidence: Buyer confirmed fit on 3 Sep 2025 demo ('it's fulfil our requirements', M. Iqbal A.R.) yet the deal has not progressed past Qualified in the 10 months since

north_star_verdict.headline: CLM evaluation stalled since the successful 3 Sep 2025 demo: buyer confirmed fit but never returned the user counts and sample contracts needed to price, tender slipped from Mar 2026 to next-year second semester, and no economic buyer has been mobilized to drive budget approval.

flags: Convert M. Iqbal A.R. from cooperative evaluator into an internal champion who will drive the budget case and tender timeline, since nothing has moved in 10 months.
Reach the economic buyer: CFO Indarto Pamungkas and Deputy CEO Nugroho Christijanto are mapped but never engaged, and internal budget approval is the explicit gate.
Manufacture a compelling event by quantifying the manual-process cost in Pupuk's own numbers; 10 months of tolerable pain (do-nothing) is the real competitor here.
best_practice_check.summary: This is an early-stage, sole-sourced SOE evaluation with strong product fit but no forcing function and no engaged power. The win levers are champion-building, reaching the economic buyer, and manufacturing urgency before a public tender opens the field.

competitive_position.summary: No live bake-off today; competition is a future public tender. Do-nothing / status quo is the strongest current threat (high): 10 months of tolerable manual-process pain with no forcing function is what is actually blocking the deal. Coupa and GEP Worldwide (both medium) are the anticipated tender entrants named in Salesforce with no buyer preference or evaluation yet; the SOE tender will bring them in when it launches, but Zycus holds early-mover advantage from a sole-sourced, validated demo. No incumbent CLM system is being replaced. Ranking by current threat: (1) do-nothing, (2) GEP Worldwide, (3) Coupa.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Listed in Salesforce Competitors__c. No buyer statements or call evidence of active evaluation; the SOE tender process will bring GEP into the shortlist when the tender launches.
competitors.how_we_win: Lean on early-mover advantage from the sole-sourced, validated demo (buyer confirmed 'fulfils our requirements', 3 Sep 2025). Wedge: Merlin AI clause library and bilingual Indonesian-English contract support as Phase 1 differentiators; secure a local reference (Bintang/Heineken) to de-risk vendor selection.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Listed in Salesforce Competitors__c. No buyer statements or call evidence of active evaluation; the SOE tender process will bring Coupa into the shortlist when the tender launches.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Certinal signing as cost-effective versus Adobe Sign/DocuSign and address Indonesian PSRE/BSRE and data-residency compliance proactively in the tender response to neutralize a global-brand rival on local-fit grounds.
